title: Peru’s Three Fingered “Aliens” (WTF Are These?!)
summary: Jesse Michels travels to Peru to investigate the controversial tridactyl (three-fingered) mummies found in the Nazca region, examining evidence from forensic experts, geneticists, and researchers who claim these beings may represent a new species or possibly extraterrestrial life. The documentary explores the scientific analysis of the bodies, the government pushback against researchers, and the complex provenance issues involving grave robbers and potential fraud.

speakers.guest: multiple guests including Dr. David Ruiz, Jose Zalce, Jaime Maussan, Elena Hardy, Alan Earas, Thierry Jamin, and others

acronyms.definition: Medical imaging technique used to scan the mummies and analyze their internal structures

acronyms.expansion: Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine
acronyms.definition: Standard file format for medical imaging data, used to share CT scan files of the mummies
Database holding genetic data including sequences from the Peruvian Nazca mummies
Modern term for UFOs used by government and military

events.description: Gravedigger Leandro discovered a cave containing over 200 tridactyl body parts and mummies in the Nazca region of Peru
French explorer Thierry Jamin met Leandro and began spearheading research on the tridactyl beings
Wooden figurines meant to resemble the Nazca mummies were confiscated at Lima airport, leading to media conflation with the actual mummies
Reuters published article claiming mummies are dolls made from animal bones with modern synthetic glue
Peruvian government began tacitly endorsing research at University of Ica on the bodies
Neil Armstrong took a BBC film crew to Ecuador looking for the alleged Tayos cave metal library

key_claims.claim: Top forensic experts in Peru, Mexico, and the United States investigated the bodies and concluded they were probably once alive
The bodies carbon date from 700 years ago to 1,800 years ago, mostly overlapping with the Nazca period
Maria's DNA showed approximately 70% known DNA and 30% completely different/unknown DNA
Metal implants in the bodies contain osmium and cadmium, extremely rare earth metals only officially discovered in the 19th century
David Grusch told Representative Eric Burlison that the physiology of some mummies is congruent with what he has seen in classified briefings
Jose Zalce spent five days in jail because the Mexican Navy accused him of being a liar about the mummies
Jaime Maussan filed a $300 million lawsuit against the Peruvian government for defamation
A GLI3 gene mutation associated with digit development disorders was found in the NCBI database containing genetic data from the mummies
One of the tridactyl beings named Monzerat appears to have a three-fingered fetus inside it
Proteomics analysis identified bone samples from one skull as alpaca bone
Two different finger bones of Maria were tested and came back as different sexes - one male, one female
